label: Squarish 2BR / 1 bath, LD style -- standard front-left, master rear-left, one common bath at the rear next to kitchen, public column splits into living (front) + dining (mid) + kitchen (rear), all stacked front-to-rear -- the solver's default LDK arrangement, no ldk_horizontal needed. Deliberately public-heavy zone ratio: 45% private (bedrooms) / 55% public (LDK + bath).

adjacencies.note: master and standard stack vertically along the left wall -- standard at the front (street side), master at the rear. Shared wall only, no door between them. Repositioned 2026-07-20 (master moved to the rear).

standard's door -- opens into living, its front-most public neighbor. Keeps the wider 1.2 m floor for door-swing clearance (same value standard has always used, now paired with living instead of dining after the 2026-07-20 reposition).

master's door -- opens into dining, its rear-most public neighbor. Keeps master's original 0.8 m floor, now paired with dining instead of living after the 2026-07-20 reposition (master moved to the rear).

common's public door -- opens into dining (its nearest public neighbor at the rear), matching its proximity to master (rear bedroom as of the 2026-07-20 reposition). DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' door-host group; a brief door_host: {"common": "kitchen"} moves the door to the kitchen wall instead, leaving this wall solid. Takes over the role common<->great played in the gr sibling.

shared plumbing wall -- kitchen and common bath stack rear-right; bath west of kitchen. ALTERNATE host of the 'common_access' group: door placed here when shared wall >= 1.40 m (clear 0.70 + frame 0.20 + guard 0.50); otherwise falls back to dining. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
living and dining stack front-to-rear -- open mouth, no wall (ld style).
dining and kitchen stack front-to-rear -- open pass-through, matches the solver's default kitchen+dining-must-stack rule directly.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_sq_side_split_bath_ld
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Floor kitchen width at 1.6 m on compact envelopes -- a modest work-triangle guard just above the PD 1096 hard floor (1.5 m). The gr sibling used 2.0 m here, but that pushes the true minimum to 11x11; 1.6 m is the highest floor that still lets 10x10 solve (9.9x9.9 is infeasible, confirmed via bisection), matching the requested minimum exactly.

notes: Created 2026-07-20 as an LDK split (living/dining/kitchen stacked
front-to-rear, the solver's default LDK arrangement) of
1s_2br_sq_side_split_bath_gr -- gr sibling deleted the same day, this
topology replaces it. Master repositioned to the rear the same day
(standard now front-left, master rear-left) -- adjacencies keep each
bedroom's original wall-width value (standard's wider 1.2 m clearance
floor, master's 0.8 m) but reassigned to whichever public room now
occupies that geometric role, same mechanism as the cl_gr->cl_ld swap.
Single common bath stays rear-anchored next to kitchen (no separate
bath band -- this is the 1-bath program, unlike the 2-bath cl_gr/cl_ld
pair which has a full-depth ensuite+common middle band); common was
never wired to a bedroom directly, so its own adjacencies (dining +
kitchen wet-core) are untouched by the reposition.
Layout intent (canonical form -- brief.carport_side='right'):
Front: [standard  | living                        ]
Rear:  [master    | common | dining->kitchen       ]
standard's door opens into living (front, mirrors its old ->great
role). master's door opens into dining (rear, mirrors its old ->great
role). common's public door goes to dining, matching its proximity to
master (now the rear bedroom). The kitchen<->common wet-core alternate
host is unchanged.
Published/canonical minimum: 10x10 (chosen deliberately per an earlier
session request, kept as-is through the master reposition). The
reposition actually tightened the solver's true floor further, to
~9.65x9.65 (9.6x9.6 infeasible, 9.65x9.65 solves -- confirmed by
bisection); 10x10 is no longer the bare minimum but is kept as the
published one since nothing asked for it to be re-tuned down again.
vs the gr sibling's 10.5x10.5. Unlike the cl_gr->cl_ld swap, this is
NOT a clean 0-warning win -- both window_area_habitable (kitchen, small
sizes only) and bath_door_into_kitchen (the door-host auto-scorer picks
kitchen over the group's dining default at every size) persist here
exactly as they did in the gr sibling at comparable sizes. The win is
purely the smaller minimum lot, not warning count. Re-verified after
the master reposition at the canonical sizes -- 10x10 and 10.5x10.5
unchanged (2 warn), 12x12 unchanged (1 warn); 11x11 briefly picked up a
second window_area_habitable warning after the reposition alone, but
the follow-up 45/55 zone-ratio change below pulled it back to 1 warn
at 11x11 -- see that paragraph.
45/55 zone ratio (same day, second follow-up): user asked for the
private column (master+standard) at 45% of the private+public total
and the public side (living+dining+kitchen+common bath) at 55% --
opposite of the solver's catalog-wide default (55/45 favoring
private, with a hard floor of private>=public that would have made
45% private outright infeasible). This needed a real solver.py change,
not just a topology flag: generalized the previously-hardcoded 50%
hard-floor / 55% soft-target into two new configurable Topology
fields, zone_ratio_private_floor_pct and zone_ratio_private_target_pct
(defaults 50.0/55.0 reproduce every other topology's behavior
byte-for-byte -- verified via full regression before touching this
topology's own values). Set here to floor=40.0 / target=45.0. Also
added a zone_balance_rooms override so common counts toward the
public side (it's zone:'service', normally neutral/excluded from the
ratio entirely) -- the user's request explicitly included the bath
on the 55% side. Verified achieved ratio lands 44-46.5% private /
53.5-56% public across 9.7x9.7 through 13x13 -- e.g. exactly 45.0%/
55.0% at 10x10 and 12x12. Side effects: true floor tightened slightly
further to ~9.6x9.6 (10x10 stays published minimum); 11x11 dropped
back to 1 warning (window_area_habitable resolved itself under the
new ratio, see paragraph above); 10x10's baseline SVG refreshed
(warn/sugg counts unchanged: 2 warn, 6 sugg, but room proportions
shifted).
Practical ceiling matches the locked 2BR/1bath floor-area knee (~13x13
/ 81 m2) -- solves through 13x13, goes non-monotonic above that (same
fixed max_area_sqm room-cap behavior documented for the gr sibling in
the squarish 2BR lot-size sweep, not a regression introduced here).
